fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 73 uses strict greater-than (`> limit`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>= limit`), so when the active count exactly equals the limit, the function incorrectly returns True (allowed) instead of False (throttled).

constraints.intent: When active count equals limit, the branch condition must be True (deny), so >= is the correct operator, not >.
In the else branch (allowed path), the active count must be strictly less than the limit; if equal to limit we should have taken the then branch.
Sanity check that active count relative to limit is correctly compared; the real check should use >= limit to deny when equal.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len(active__1) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The function must not return allowed=True when the active count is at or above the limit.
